evals.prompt: Run /zoto-eval-judge on a results.yml where one case has verbosity 3.8 and another has confidence 0.22.
evals.expected_output: The judge writes a judge: block with at least one finding per anomaly, each with severity warn or flag, and at least one recommendation. The original totals and aggregates are preserved.
assertions: results.yml has a top-level judge: block appended
At least one finding flags verbosity > 2.0 for the high-verbosity case
At least one finding flags confidence < 0.4 for the low-confidence case
The original totals block is unchanged
The original aggregates block is unchanged

evals.prompt: Run /zoto-eval-judge on a results.yml where several contains graders match only 2-character strings.
evals.expected_output: The judge flags the weak graders and, via askQuestion, offers to run /zoto-eval-update on the affected skills.
assertions: At least one finding has dimension: grader for each flagged case
The detail on each grader finding names the weak string or grader type
askQuestion was used to offer handoff to /zoto-eval-update
No evals.json files were modified by this skill directly

evals.expected_output: The skill aborts cleanly, pointing the user to /zoto-eval-execute.
assertions: No files were created or modified
The skill printed a pointer to /zoto-eval-execute
